memory.summary: I’m a bit squeezed right now: the quiz is due Thursday morning, the team is still meeting tonight, and Assignment A is only halfway done. With my energy low and stress up, this feels like a tight overlap.
I’m still in that same tight overlap, and it feels heavier now with my energy really low and stress up. Nothing new changed, but the load is starting to feel more urgent.
팀원이 회의 자료를 물어봐서, 지금 내 준비 상태가 곧바로 드러날 상황이 됐다. 에너지가 낮은데도 팀 일정 압박이 더 선명해졌다.
I still feel stuck in the same overlap, with low energy and more stress than I’d like. Nothing new changed, but the pressure is just sitting there in the background.
Nothing new has changed, but the overlap still feels heavier with my energy this low and stress creeping up. It’s starting to feel more urgent than just background pressure.
I’m still in the same spot: low energy, a lot of sleep debt, and the team pressure hasn’t really gone away. It feels more exhausting now than before, even without anything new happening.
Nothing new changed, but I’m still carrying the same overlap with even less energy and a lot of sleep debt. It feels heavier now, like the stress is just compounding in the background.
Nothing new has changed, but I feel more worn down now with the same pressure still hanging around. The low energy and sleep debt are making everything feel sharper and harder to shrug off.
Nothing new has changed, but I’m noticing the same pressure feels even heavier with my energy this low. The sleep debt is making the stress harder to ignore.
Nothing new has changed, but I feel even more drained now, and the same pressure is starting to weigh on me harder. The sleep debt and stress are both still very present.

desires: not look flaky or let my team think I’m dropping the ball on the 19:00 meeting
avoid falling further behind on the Wed assignment while I’m already running on low energy
not make the Thursday quiz situation worse by procrastinating tonight
get through the next few hours without crashing so hard that tomorrow feels miserable

goals.description: Send teammate_A a quick confirmation that the meeting materials are basically ready, then do one last fast check before the 19:00 team meeting so I don’t look unreliable.
goals.rationale: Protecting team trust is the nearest social priority, and a brief check is enough without getting stuck overpolishing.

goals.description: Make one focused push on Assignment A toward a clearly submit-ready state, but stop once the highest-impact parts are done so I don’t turn tired effort into sloppy work.
goals.rationale: Assignment A is the hardest academic deadline, so I want meaningful progress now without burning out or wasting focus.

goals.description: Do a short first pass on Thursday quiz prep after the meeting and assignment work, just enough to keep tomorrow from starting at zero.
goals.rationale: I want some control back on the quiz, but it should stay contained so tonight stays balanced and manageable.

goals.description: Wrap up early enough to get meaningful rest and keep tomorrow from feeling heavier than it already will.
goals.rationale: Sleep and recovery matter because I’m already carrying sleep debt and stress, and I don’t want to trade tonight’s effort for a worse tomorrow.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 by keeping teammate_A in the loop; I’m weighing that against assignment_A, but the quick confirmation protects trust and only costs a moment before I switch to work.

goals.description: Finish Assignment A to a solid submit-ready state before midnight, aiming to reduce deadline pressure and avoid looking unreliable.
goals.rationale: Academic work is a top priority for me, and it’s already half done with a hard deadline tonight. I want to protect trust with teammate_A too, so getting this stabilized matters most.

goals.description: Prepare enough for the Thursday 9:00 AM quiz to avoid walking in cold, even if it’s just a focused first pass tonight.
goals.rationale: The quiz is urgent but not as immediate as Assignment A. I should start it soon so I’m not cramming tomorrow, since my focus and sleep are both limited.

goals.description: Attend and contribute to the 19:00 team meeting so I don’t seem flaky or disconnected.
goals.rationale: Relationships matter a lot to me, and the meeting is fixed for tonight. Keeping steady with the team helps protect trust without overwhelming the rest of the evening.

goals.description: Stop work early enough to get a reasonable sleep, rather than sacrificing tomorrow for one more hour tonight.
goals.rationale: I care about health and focus, but this only becomes active once the urgent deadlines are handled. I don’t want to dig the sleep debt deeper than necessary.
